body.title: Intent extractor as the system's canonical learning substrate
body.text:
# D-intent-extractor-learning-substrate-2026-06-14
**title:** Intent extractor as the system's canonical learning substrate
**date:** 2026-06-14
**status:** open
**spawned_tickets:** T-intent-extractor-device, T-intent-extractor-backfill, T-intent-extractor-librarian, T-consequence-intent-extractor

## Decision narrative
The system has no canonical learning substrate — every device that learns does it privately and incompatibly. The intent extractor is a new callable device (devices/intent/) with a three-method API (predict/validate/patterns) that all learning passes through. V1 learning mechanism is store-and-retrieve: every validate() call persists a prediction+outcome pair to devlab.validations; predict() retrieves top-K validated examples as few-shot context. The nullable prediction_id design allows post-hoc ground-truth deposits without a prior prediction — the Librarian uses only this path (never predict(), which would produce noise on genuine unknowns). Wiring into cc_queue.py add at decoration time (fail-open) and Librarian research completion completes the substrate.

## Hypothesis
Systems that route all learning through a shared intent extractor will improve prediction accuracy measurably over successive validation rounds, as measured by held-out accuracy delta after 20 domain-specific validations vs. baseline.

## Measurement Signal
predict() accuracy on 10 held-out tickets after 20 validation signals in the same domain must exceed baseline (0 validations) accuracy — measured during T-intent-extractor-device close as the learning delta completion criterion.

## Goal Link
G-intent-extractor (new goal: build intention extraction as the system's fundamental learning substrate)

## Context
- Emerged from design conversation 2026-06-14
- Supersedes T-intent-extractor-agent (M, design) which was too narrow
- Librarian exception is explicit by design: research = genuine unknowns; predicting what research will find = noise; validate(prediction_id=None) is the post-hoc path only
- Gates on T-devlab-schema-create (the immediate unblocking ticket from D-storage-layer-formalization-2026-06-14)
- The Radar O'Reilly pattern: goal state = system anticipates before asked, built from validated predictions in every domain (coding, people, projects, calendar)
- Validated predictions = compile targets in every domain — this is what makes the learning substrate universal
